repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest Stat entries are stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap and stat_dose_response_minimax, which address different estimands.

published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 — longitudinal g-formula/backward-regression identification and sequential double robustness.
LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 — sequential longitudinal remainder framework, but not the proposed direct-backward HOIF identity.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap is a matched causal minimax rate but for offline-policy regret, not a longitudinal g-formula.

published_prior_art.cite: BonviniKennedyKeele2025 — two-time-point g-formula HOIF estimators with minimax-optimal rates in some anisotropic/additive models; RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR also states HOIF results applicable to time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding.
RobinsTchetgenLiVanderVaart2009 — two-convex-mixture lower-bound method for multiparameter semiparametric functionals; the normalized transition-mediated implementation is not shown there.
No cited paper establishes this particular transition-smoothness corollary; it is a consequence of the proposed frontier rather than an independent method.
RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 develops higher-order estimating equations, but not a necessary-and-sufficient scalar-versus-multi-index order frontier for this recursive construction.
RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 — single-time missing-response HOIF slow-rate benchmark after the transition channel is removed.

structure_flags.label: thm:attaining-estimator and thm:chained-converse
structure_flags.one_line: The unproved matched minimax frontier is the kernel but is labeled as two Theorems; promote it to an explicit combined Conjecture and retain supporting lemmas/propositions beneath it.

novelty_flags.label: thm:attaining-estimator / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: The review omits RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR (arXiv:1601.05820), which explicitly says its monotone-missingness HOIF results apply to time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding; the proposed transition-L1-Hölder class and rate must be separated from it.

soundness_flags.label: lem:direct-bias
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed bias identity omits the residual E_{P_{H_1}}[(pi_1/hat pi_1) E_g[{1-pi_2/hat pi_2}(hat m_2-m_2)|H_1]]; its second term equals the displayed Q_2 term only when hat pi_1=pi_1, so the claimed absence of a first-period transport product is false.

soundness_flags.label: ass:outcome-state-sufficiency
soundness_flags.one_line: Exact terminal-outcome state sufficiency is load-bearing, but the generic physiologic-challenge description supplies no named protocol or validation demonstrating that finite H_2 retains all earlier-history information relevant to Y.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Correct the direct-bias expansion, then redefine the transport correction chain against its actual weighted transition residual and restate the rate conjecture only for that corrected estimator.
Add RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016TR and give a precise assumption-by-assumption and rate comparison with its time-dependent-treatment application and with BonviniKennedyKeele2025.
Anchor outcome-state sufficiency in a named two-decision protocol, specify the state-construction rule, and provide a credible design or validation argument for the omitted-history exclusion.
Recast the matched upper/lower minimax statement as the headline Conjecture, with the estimator and fuzzy experiments as its committed construction route.

recommended_next_step: Revise the load-bearing remainder identity and its HOIF construction, establish a concrete comparator separation, and ground the state-sufficiency condition before another novelty review.
